canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Self-Instruct is consistently positioned as an instrument for evaluating language models, frequently appearing in lists of benchmarks alongside Vicuna, Koala, and Dolly Eval. Its primary purpose is described as testing model performance, specifically targeting generalization, response quality, and the capabilities of instruction-tuned models. There is notable disagreement in the definitions regarding its composition; most describe it as a dataset based on "self-instructed prompts" generated by a model itself, while one paper defines it as a test set of "human-generated instructions". A less common framing also presents Self-Instruct not only as an evaluation tool but as a "framework... for improving the instruction-following capabilities" of models. In terms of what it assesses, papers use Self-Instruct to measure the construct of Commonsense knowledge.
papers: GIFT-SW:Gaussian noise Injected Fine-Tuning of Salient Weights forLLMs
OneQuantLLMforALL: Fine-tuning QuantizedLLMs Once for Efficient Deployments
TUNA: Comprehensive Fine-grained Temporal Understanding Evaluation on Dense Dynamic Videos
Toward Automatic Discovery of a Canine Phonetic Alphabet
Exploring the Hidden Capacity ofLLMs for One-Step Text Generation
Judge and Improve: Towards a Better Reasoning of Knowledge Graphs with Large Language Models

definitions.text: An evaluation dataset based on self-instructed prompts, used to test model generalization and response quality across a wide range of tasks.
definitions.paper: TUNA: Comprehensive Fine-grained Temporal Understanding Evaluation on Dense Dynamic Videos

definitions.text: A framework and dataset for improving the instruction-following capabilities of language models by using the model itself to generate new instructions, inputs, and outputs.
definitions.paper: Toward Automatic Discovery of a Canine Phonetic Alphabet

definitions.text: A test set of human-generated instructions from various tasks and sources used to evaluate the performance of instruction-tuned models.
definitions.paper: Judge and Improve: Towards a Better Reasoning of Knowledge Graphs with Large Language Models

relationships.paper: DA-KD: Difficulty-Aware Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Large Language Models

icml25: DA-KD: Difficulty-Aware Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Large Language Models
